my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished. Added 'transformers' param to Encoding.encode() signature (new kwarg after disallowed_special), a docstring blurb, and a small block right after the docstring (before 'if allowed_special == "all":') that applies the transformer(s) to text. No other lines touched. Diff:
--- a/tiktoken/core.py
+++ b/tiktoken/core.py
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@ from __future__ import annotations
 import functools
 from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
-from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, AbstractSet, Collection, Literal, NoReturn, Sequence
+from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, AbstractSet, Callable, Collection, Literal, NoReturn, Sequence
@@ -82,6 +82,7 @@ class Encoding:
         allowed_special: ... = set(),
         disallowed_special: ... = "all",
+        transformers: Callable[[str], str] | Sequence[Callable[[str], str]] | None = None,
     ) -> list[int]:
@@ +docstring blurb+example
     ):
+        if transformers is not None:
+            if callable(transformers):
+                transformers = [transformers]
+            for transformer in transformers:
+                text = transformer(text)
+
         if allowed_special == "all":
             ...
You're clear to re-read core.py fresh and add return_positions now.
